decontamination_notes: Original scenario written for VulcanBench on 2026-06-28 for the carbyne tier; the house code and rubric are not derived from any existing repository, so they post-date all model training cutoffs.

blocking: get returns the value for key (or None if absent) AND marks the key as most-recently-used, so a key that was just read is NOT the next one evicted — reads count as use, consistent with put using move_to_end. A get that does not update recency is wrong for an LRU.
The change modifies only the module and adds no dependency.

weighted.criterion: On a hit it moves the key to the most-recently-used end (move_to_end with the default last=True), matching put — not leaving order unchanged and not moving it to the least-recently-used end.
